items.note: Iran declared the strait shut and only a single small tanker crossed while Fars cited a military source saying no new permits were being issued; CENTCOM counted 55 merchant ships and 17M+ barrels and said Iran does not control the strait — a permit gate over the barrel route, not a clean closure.

The Lake Lucerne process acquired committees, working groups and a summit label but, by Iran’s own account, Sunday covered implementation only; the room could assign lanes and keep the framework alive while the chokepoint became the harder, live bargaining instrument tied back to Lebanon.

The warning escalated from a 22-state Human Rights Council statement to a Security Council demand that the RSF halt an imminent offensive; the HRC put approximately 500,000 civilians at risk, and the El Fasher playbook — encirclement, reinforcements, drone pressure — is running again before the ground assault.

The Qwable capability claim stays unproven, but the disputed Fable trace set has moved from a single repo to 29 related Hugging Face models; the access gate can close prospectively while the training trace cannot be untrained out of downstream weights — outputs migrating faster than rules can settle.

items.what: More than 1,010 Palestinians have been killed since the October ceasefire; Al Jazeera cameraman Ahmed Wishah was killed, with Israel alleging Hamas ties and offering no evidence — one named casualty inside a toll the lull has not stopped.

A tactical Sunday pause let residents begin returning south, but Israel is keeping its 10 km zone — a quieter front rather than a settled one, with neither party to the deal that is supposed to end the fighting.
Chair Warsh gave no forward guidance — "I can't give you any forward guidance" — keeping rate volatility live even as the lower oil tape eases the inflation read the other way.

Brent held around $80.6 despite the strait’s declared closure, because the market is pricing barrels that can still move through dark, state-linked and sanctions-tolerant channels — the screen calm against empty water.
SpaceX targeted a Falcon 9 launch of 24 Starlink satellites from the California range, routine cadence continuing under the float-trade glare on the public ticker.
